qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Command injection via shell=True in poetry run on Windows
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes command injection via shell=True in base_env.py:457-467 on Windows. The advisory describes path traversal in wheel_installer.py:47,59 where untrusted wheel entry paths are joined without containment. Same package (Poetry) but different vulnerability class (command injection vs path traversal), different vulnerable code (base_env.py vs wheel_installer.py), and different root cause (shell=True enabling metacharacter interpretation vs missing path containment in wheel destination construction).

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Arbitrary file write via path traversal in tarfile/zipfile extraction during package installation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies path traversal in helpers.py:410-426 extractall() function used during sdist/package preparation (chef.py:104, info.py:322). The advisory identifies path traversal in wheel_installer.py:47,59 WheelDestination class used during wheel installation via executor.py:607. While both are path traversal vulnerabilities in Poetry (CWE-22), the vulnerable code, root cause, and source-to-sink paths are different: extractall() in helpers.py lacks archive member path validation, while WheelDestination in wheel_installer.py constructs destination paths by joining untrusted wheel entry names without resolve()+is_relative_to() containment. The finding's PoC exercises the extractall helper, not the WheelDestination class.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory describes path traversal in Poetry's wheel_installer.py WheelDestination class (lines 47 and 59), where untrusted wheel entry paths are joined to build destination paths without resolve()+is_relative_to() containment, reachable via executor.py:607. The tarfile_path_traversal finding covers a different path traversal in helpers.py extractall() (lines 410-426) used during sdist preparation via chef.py and info.py. These are distinct vulnerable code paths with different root causes. The shell_injection_windows finding is a command injection vulnerability in a different code component (base_env.py shell=True).
evidence: Advisory specifies vulnerable code at src/poetry/installation/wheel_installer.py:47 and :59 (WheelDestination class)
Advisory PoC uses WheelDestination to install a crafted wheel with ../../pwned.txt entry
tarfile_path_traversal finding identifies src/poetry/utils/helpers.py:410-426 (extractall function) as vulnerable code
tarfile_path_traversal PoC exercises helpers.py extractall() with a malicious tarball, not wheel_installer.py WheelDestination
shell_injection_windows finding identifies src/poetry/utils/env/base_env.py:457-467 (shell=True on Windows) as vulnerable code
